sections_cited: Section 23 of the Sindh Civil Servants Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1077 of 2023 C. P No. D – 1319 of 2024 C. P No. D – 1396 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (Priority) 1. For hearing of CMA No.4667/2023 (S/A) 2. For hearing of main case 20-10-2025 M/s Achar Khan Gabol and Ghulam Ali Bozdar, Advocates for the Petitioners Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Additional Advocate General Sindh along with Hafiz Lal Muhammad Tunio, District Education Officer (ES&HS), Ghotki >>>>>..<<<<< After arguing the matter at some length, learned counsel for the Petitioners submits that the Petitioners against the impugned Notification dated 21-12-2022 (available at pages 133 to 143 in CP No.D-1077 of 2023) issued by Respondent No.1/ Secretary, Education and Literacy Department, Government of Sindh, the Petitioners have already preferred a joint Departmental Appeal under Section 23 of the Sindh Civil Servants Act, 1973 Read with Rule 3 of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appeal) Rules, 1980 before the Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh, and the same has not yet been decided. He further contended that the Petitioners would be satisfied and will not press these Petitions, if the Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh is directed to decide the Appeal of the Petitioners, if pending, within a shortest possible time. Learned Addl.AG does not oppose such proposal of learned counsel for the Petitioners. Accordingly, the instant Petitions are disposed of with direction to Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh to decide the aforesaid Appeal of the Petitioners (available at pages 133 to 143 in C.P No.D-1077 of 2023) after providing an opportunity of hearing to the Petitioners and the concerned Officers within thirty days in accordance with the law, under compliance report to this Court through Additional Registrar.

Learned Addl.AG does not oppose such proposal of learned counsel for the Petitioners. Accordingly, the instant Petitions are disposed of with direction to Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh to decide the aforesaid Appeal of the Petitioners (available at pages 133 to 143 in C.P No.D-1077 of 2023) after providing an opportunity of hearing to the Petitioners and the concerned Officers within thirty days in accordance with the law, under compliance report to this Court through Additional Registrar. Office to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matters. Judge Judge ARBROHI/PS
